headline: Peacock Doubles Down on Crime Drama with 'M.I.A.', a Family Vengeance Powder Keg
lede: Ozark co-creator Bill Dubuque delivers another pulse-pounding narrative with Peacock's latest South Florida crime saga that promises high-stakes family drama and ruthless retribution.
body:
Peacock is positioning itself as a premium destination for gritty crime storytelling with the upcoming series 'M.I.A.', a propulsive family revenge drama that draws inspiration from telenovela intensity and modern prestige thriller sensibilities.

The series centers on Etta Tiger Jonze (Shannon Gisela), a protagonist thrust into a high-stakes revenge narrative after experiencing a devastating personal tragedy. Dubuque's trademark narrative complexity suggests audiences can expect multilayered character motivations and morally nuanced storytelling that transcends traditional crime drama tropes.

South Florida serves as more than just a backdrop, emerging as a critical narrative environment where familial bonds and criminal networks intersect with explosive potential. The series appears poised to explore themes of justice, survival, and the transformative power of personal agency in the face of systemic violence.

With Dubuque's proven track record from 'Ozark', 'M.I.A.' represents another ambitious exploration of how ordinary individuals navigate extraordinary criminal circumstances. The show signals Peacock's continued investment in distinctive, character-driven crime narratives that prioritize psychological depth over simplistic genre conventions.
key_facts: Bill Dubuque's second major crime drama series
